entry.title: Re: Asset transfer speed degrading (ABUMN slow)
entry.content:
Could you consider using a BAPI

BAPIs - SAP Documentation

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am from the USA, and I am fairly new to SAP. I am excited to be a part of this group and to learn more!

entry.title: Re: What in ILX does the "Accomplishments" menu do (and h...
entry.content:
@Kristian_Unell, I believe the Accomplishments link in the Activities mini menu refers to Learning Accomplishments, which is a feature in the Learning module that was released several years ago. Seeing that option in the Activities menu means that the Access Learning Accomplishments workflow has been added to the USER Learning security role assigned to the test user that you were using in your test system.

To confirm in your test system, view the permissions assigned to the User Learning security role(s) that your company uses. In Learning Admin > System Admin > Security > Role Management, there are two default User roles in called DEFAULT USER and LEARNING_USER. In addition, during implementation, our partner created a copy of the default User role and named it something similar, so you may need to review all of the User roles to find the one that has the "Access Learning Accomplishments" workflow assigned (the workflow lives in the Learning group). Note: If your company has added the Role field to the User entity configuration, you can view the People > Users record of the test user to see what security role is assigned to that test user.

Once you determine which User role contains the Access Learning Accomplishments permission, remove that permission from the role. Log out of Learning and then login again to confirm that the Accomplishments link has been removed from the Activities menu on the ILX.

CAVEAT: I would strongly recommend you review all of the User roles that are being actively used in Production to determine whether this workflow has been assigned to any of them. It may be that the workflow was never removed from the User role in Test but was not actually included in the User role in Production.

Good luck,
Mary Katherine

entry.title: Re: How to apply metadata patch in SAP Service and Asset Manager
entry.content: Good job!!

entry.title: MTO Production order execution, with variable GR qty for sales order with lot charge
entry.content:
The business process is transforming customer supplied semiconductor wafers into individual shipping components.

Key elements:

  1. Sales orders are billed as a lot charge, with a sales order unit of one. (It is this way due the customer requirements and purchase order)
    1. Regardless of the resulting good parts, the customer pays for processing all of them.
  2. The associated MTO production order needs to be quantity of one, as the stock quantity needs to match the sales order qty.
  3. However, the lower-level production orders, have quantities greater than one, as all the stock is at the individual component level. (this is typical with wafers, as each wafer has a known variable good qty)
    1. As a result, we have to “write off” 201 the excess stock and only post quantity of one to the MTO production order.

The ask: does SAP provide any functionality to manage this type of business process, without a bunch of transactional work arounds?

entry.title: Unlock the Future of Compliance: Join the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024!
entry.content:
As global regulations continue to evolve, staying ahead in the compliance landscape is crucial for businesses of all sizes. The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024 is your gateway to mastering the latest trends, technologies, and strategies in the world of compliance for electronic documents processing and statutory reporting. Building on the success of previous years, this year’s virtual summit promises to be another unique opportunity for deeper insights, interactive sessions, and networking opportunities. 

Why Attend: In this two-day virtual event, you will learn from SAP experts, network with industry professionals, and discover how SAP Document and Reporting Compliance can help your business go global confidently.   

Whether you are navigating new regulations, digitalizing your reporting and electronic documents processes, or simply looking to stay informed about the latest trends, this summit has something for everyone. 

  • Exclusive Insights: Gain knowledge from SAP experts about upcoming road map features and innovations in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 
  • Interactive Sessions: Participate in live demos and Q&A sessions designed to give you practical knowledge. 
  • Networking Opportunities: Gain insights from SAP customers currently implementing and using the solution by hearing their experiences, connect with peers, industry experts, and share your own experiences. 
  • Future-Focused Content: Explore SAP Document and Reporting Compliance powered by AI and how you can leverage these automated features to drive your business forward. 

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024 is more than just an event— it is a community of forward-thinkers dedicated to navigating the worldwide complexities of compliance with confidence and innovation. Join us this year and take your strategy for electronic documents processing and statutory reporting to the next level! 

Join us virtually on September 18 and 19 from 2:00 to 6:00 p.m. CEST for the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024. 

Register now and secure your spot. We cannot wait to meet you there! 

 

                                 Register now

 

This virtual event will be conducted in English, and registration is free of charge. If you have questions, feel free to leave a comment below, our team will be glad to get back to you. 

 About the solution 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is SAP’s solution to empower businesses to fulfill local legal requirements in many countries around the globe. The solution acts as a central hub to enable compliance with electronic invoicing, electronic reporting, statutory reporting and audit requirements worldwide, while standardizing and simplifying operational processes end-to-end. Learn more here.

entry.title: Is it possible to mass update Equipments with the Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

By using transaction IE05 (Change Equipment - Mass Change), we noticed that it's not possible to update the same fields of multiple equipments with different values for each field.

Considering this, we'd like to know the following:

  • Is it possible to mass update equipments with the Migration Cockpit (Migrate Your Data app) as we've described?

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,
Expert team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to mass update Equipments with the Mig...
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In regards to the app's functionalities, it's not possible to mass update or change equipments with the Migration Cockpit.

The Migrate Your Data app (Migration Cockpit) was designed only to migrate the initial data required on an SAP S/4HANA system. Further data loads besides the first one(s) or mass changes and updates to already existing data aren't the purpose of the app, so, consequently, it also can't update the data maintained for equipments in the system. For mass changes, the respective business area application (or transaction) must be used, as IE05.

More information about the Migration Cockpit's designed purpose and standard functionalities can be read in the KBA below:

  • 2684818 - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit usage for mass processing data or as interface

I hope this answers your question!

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Can a delete or update query be run against a datasphere table?
entry.content: Hi. I have the same problem. What do you mean by "Open schema" ?

entry.title: Re: How does Half day holiday displayed in table TFACS
entry.content:
Hi Shlomi, np. better late then never :).

Just to be sure, you need to have the same values as in MON01..MON02 as half days? 

MON01..MON12 have each 31 chars, for each day one char: 1-working day / 0 - notworking day. For me half-day is the same as working day - so every '1' in the MON01...MON12 columns stays for working day, either half or full.

Do you need to have a difference between first-half or second-half day? If not, I would just duplicate the MON01...MON12 1:1, and when calculating with the duplicated columns, just divide by 2. 

Martin

entry.title: One of my CDS CQL statements doesn't change the backing postgre-sql database
entry.content:
In my Core Data Services project (running locally but with --hybrid switch to 'connect to' a BTP destination and connected to a postgre-sql database also running locally in a docker container), I have several cql statements, three of which are UPDATE statements.  All but two of the cql statements 'work', including one of the UPDATE statements.  However, two of the UPDATE statements don't seem to update the backing postgre-sql database.

All of the statements are using '.run()' directly on the cds object (none are creating transactions).  My understanding is that cds.run() creates and then (on success) commits a transaction.

Below is the code being run for one of the UPDATE statements that doesn't update postgre-sql.  The two SELECT statements are there to help me diagnose.  When the code runs, the first SELECT shows null for submissionBegunAt.  After the UPDATE runs, the second SELECT shows the date that was assigned during the UPDATE run, for submissionBegunAt.

However, after all the code runs, the database doesn't reflect the changes.

CODE THAT 'DOESN'T WORK'

const markBegunAt = async () => {
let affectedGroup = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
SELECT(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.where(
{
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
}
)
);

console.log(affectedGroup);
//const tx = _closureVariables.cds.transaction();
//_closureVariables.cds
try {
const result = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
UPDATE(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.set({submissionBegunAt: new Date().toISOString()})
.where({
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
})
);

console.log(result);
//await tx.commit();
}
catch(exception) {
console.log(exception);
//await tx.rollback();
}


affectedGroup = await _closureVariables.cds.run(
SELECT(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.where(
{
request_formNumber: lineItem.request_formNumber,
moduleRole_module_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_module_ID)},
moduleRole_ID: {in: lineItem.roles.map(r => r.moduleRole_ID)}
}
)
);

console.log(affectedGroup);

And below is the code for the UPDATE that's 'making it to the backing postgre-sql database'

CODE THAT WORKS

UPDATE(_closureVariables.entities.Roles)
.set({status: "A"})
.where({
request_formNumber: req.data.requestId,
moduleRole_ID: req.data.roleId,
moduleRole_module_ID: req.data.moduleId
})
 
I don't see any substantive difference in these pieces of code.
 
I appreciate any help!

entry.title: Re: One of my CDS CQL statements doesn't change the backing postgre-sql database
entry.content: I didn't copy the 'CODE THAT WORKS' correctly. Here's all of the relevant code. Thanks!

Sample JSON:

{

    "orderNum": "A987654321",

    "customerName": "John Doe"

}

 

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.content: @harjisinghDoes it support SAP Datasphere analytical model built using a remote table. I dont see it working. Please clarify.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere- نمذجة البيانات
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere تحقيق أقصى استفادة من بيانات الأعمال
 
 نموذجك الأول وتقريرك الأول

. هو حل متكامل لإدارة البيانات وتحليلها، يتيح للمؤسسات تنظيم وتجميع وتحليل بياناتها من مصادر متعددة في بيئة واحدة متكاملة

مما يوفر أدوات قوية لتصميم نماذج البيانات، وإنشاء تقارير تحليلية متقدمة وتنفيذ عمليات استخراج وتحويل وتحميل البيانات و يساعد المؤسسات على  اتخاذ قرارات مستنيرة بناءً على بيانات دقيقة وشاملة

Datasphere هذه المقالة موجهة للمبتدئين الذين يرغبون في معرفة كيفية استهلاك البيانات من

Datasphere  وستتبع هذه المقالة سلسلة من المدونات للحصول على فهم كامل  حول

نموذجك وتقريرك الأول

:  بدايةً، لنعرف ما هو منشئ البيانات. يقدم منشئ البيانات مجموعة من المحررات لإنشاء العناصر في طبقة البيانات مثل
  الجداول-
  العرض الرسومي-
SQL  عرض-
إنشاء هيئة جديدة-
  النموذج التحليلي-
 تدفق البيانات-
  البحث الذكي-
تدفق النسخ المتماثل-
 سلسلة المهام-

منشئ البيانات هو أداة تتيح للمستخدمين إنشاء وتعديل وإدارة مجموعة متنوعة من العناصر في طبقة البيانات. تشمل هذه الأدوات محررات متنوعة تساعد على تصميم الجداول، إنشاء العروض الرسومية ،  تحديد العلاقات بين البيانات، بناء النماذج التحليلية، وتنفيذ عمليات البحث الذكية

:الخطوة الأولى 

يمكنك استيراد الملفات, تقوم بتحديد ملف المصدر من جهاز الكمبيوتر الخاص بك. في هذه الحالة، الملف الذي قمت بتحضيره 

                                                                                                    :  الخطوة الثانية  

نبقي كل شيء كما هو ونضغط على نشر  وتعطيه اسمًا، في حالتنا، "طلب مبيعات" وتقوم بنشره.  النشريعني أننا نقوم فعليًا بإنشاء الجدول على قاعدة البيانات   

بعد نشر الجدول بنجاح، ستجد الجدول في نظرة عامة بمنشئ البيانات. دعنا نضغط عليه. وهكذا نصل إلى محرر الجدول

: الخطوة الثالثة 

نحتاج إلى تعيين معرّف "رقم طلب مبيعات"كحقل مفتاح، ثم نشر الجدول. لذا يمكننا الآن أيضًا عرض البيانات في المعاينة بعد نشر الجدول ستظهر هذه البيانات في أسفل الصفحة، ستستغرق ثانية، ثم يمكنك معاينة البيانات التي قمنا بتحميلها مباشرةً ,يتيح لك محرر الجدول تعريف الجداول ودلالاتها 

:الخطوة الرابعة

 الآن بعد استيراد الملف ونشر الجدول المحلي، يمكنك استخدامه لنمذجة إضافية. لنقم بإنشاء  عرض بسيط ليتم استخدامه  لإعداد تقرير بسيط . وهنا أيضًا، في نظرة عامة منشئ البيانات، نضغط على زر إنشاء عرض رسومي جديد

تظهر لوحة فارغة الآن، مع نافذة على اليسار لمستودعك وملفات المصدر. نفتح الجداول ونرى جدول طلب مبيعات الذي قمنا بتحميله للتو

لنقم بسحبه إلى اللوحة. والآن يظهر قسم الخصائص إلى يمين الشاشة. نضغط على خصائص المقطع. نعطيه اسمًا، مثل "روئية البيانات  لطلب مبيعات". نظرًا لأننا نريد استخدامه للتقارير، نحدد الاستخدام الدلالي لمجموعة بيانات تحليلية. تحتاج أيضًا إلى عرض الاستهلاك بحيث يكون مرئيًا للاستخدامات الخارجية. ستحصل على رسالة خطأ تفيد بأنه لم يتم تعريف أي مقاييس بعد. لذا، لنأخذها هنا: المبلغ الإجمالي، المبلغ الصافي، ومبلغ الضريبة، نميزها ونسحبها ببساطة إلى قسم المقايس

.الآن يمكنك حفظ ونشر هذا العرض. ولا تنس نشره لكي نتمكن من المتابعة

:الخطوة الخامسة

الآن أنت في الخطوة الأخيرة لإنشاء تقرير بسيط للتحقق من نموذج البيانات الخاص بك. نستخدم زر تبديل التطبيق في الزاوية العلوية اليمنى

SAP Analytics Cloud  للتبديل نذهب إلى 

نذهب إلى القصص، وننشئ  تقرير جديدة، يمكنك إضافة رسم بياني وستظهر نافذة منبثقة تطلب منك الاتصال بنموذج البيانات الخاص بك 

علينا تحديد مقياس, لقد حددنا ثلاثة مقاييس: المبلغ الإجمالي، المبلغ الصافي، ومبلغ الضريبة. لذا دعنا نأخذ المبلغ الصافي. نريد تقريرًا بناءً على منظمات المبيعات. الآن قمنا بإنشاء تقريرنا الأول. يمكنك حفظه. وبهذا نكون قد انتهينا من الجزء الأول 

بشكل عام، هناك خيارات مختلفة لاستهلاك البيانات  تقدم اتصالًا مباشرًا حيًا، وهذا ما رأيناه في مقالنا، ابق على اتصال مع المقالة التالية لتتعلم كيفية 

.الاتصال بمصدر بعيد وإضافة البعد الزمني

entry.title: Re: SAP ByDesign webservice : Query Employee Actual times
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Hope you're doing well.

By checking this scenario further in the SAP Business ByDesign, I was able to find the webservice "Manage Employee Actual Times" in Communication Arrangement "Time Administration Integration".

This can be checked by following the steps below:

  1. Go to the Application and User Management work center.
  2. Access the Communication Arrangement work center view.
  3. Select the communication scenario named Time Administration Integration.
  4. Click the Edit button.
  5. Navigate to the Technical Data tab.
  6. Click the Edit Advanced Settings button.
  7. Select the Manage Employee Actual Times service in the Inbound Communications table, then click Download WSDL.
  8. Open the WSDL in a API testing software (such as Postman).

You mentioned that you are facing an error message, if you are using the WSDL from the steps above already, then please raise a case to SAP Support so we can evaluate the root cause of the error message.

Thanks and best regards,
Saulo.
SAP Product Support

entry.content: Thanks very much

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024
entry.content:
More and more authorities are exploring the benefits of a fully digital reporting and electronic invoicing system. With multiple levels of regulations to adhere to, it is crucial for enterprises to stay up-to-date with the latest regulatory trends and resources to enable compliance. SAP can help you on this journey. 

We are excited to announce the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024, where you will have the opportunity to explore SAP's solution for electronic document processing and statutory reporting: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

We will discuss the solution's roadmap and best practices to assist you in complying with ever-changing legal regulations worldwide. Secure your spot and be part of a global conversation on how to achieve compliance excellence and drive your business forward with SAP. Learn more about the event here. 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Virtual Summit 2024
When: September 18 and 19

8:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m. EDT

9:00 a.m. – 1:00 p.m. BRT

2:00 p.m. – 6:00 p.m CEST

5:30 p.m. – 9:30 p.m IST

Where: Virtually – join us from anywhere!

Agenda:

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance – today and on the roadmap

  • Learn more about SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and its upcoming innovations​
  • Receive information on the latest on the roadmap​
  • Understand what SAP’s strategy means for you as a customer
  • Learn how customers are succeeding with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Provide SAP with your individual feedback

Deep dive with experts – breakout sessions on:

  • Architecture and solution offering
  • Cross-applications within SAP
  • Customer and partner testimonials
  • ​What's new in Global Tax Management overall?​
  • Many other related relevant topics

Register here

entry.title: Connecting BPC to External ERP System (SAGE)
entry.content: Is there any standard documentation for how to connect SAP BPC to a non-SAP ERP system? I'm struggling to find any in depth guides, and a client is working on upgrading their non-SAP ERP system, but we want to test the integration with BPC as well.

entry.title: First Option in RadioButtonGroup is undefined when selected
entry.content:
I have a RadioButtonGroup in my form that is bound to my OData model as follows:

<RadioButtonGroup id="OnsiteRemote" columns="4" enabled="true" selectedIndex="{/OnsiteRemote}">
  <RadioButton id="idRemote" text="Remote" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd" />
  <RadioButton id="idOnsite" text="Onsite" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd"/>
  <RadioButton id="idOnsitePreferred" text="Onsite Preferred" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd"/>
  <RadioButton id="idCombination" text="Combination"/>
</RadioButtonGroup>

If I select the first option, "Remote", and submit the form the field is coming in as undefined. This does not happen if I pick any of the other 3 options and submit, OR if I pick one of the other options first and then go back and select "Remote". 

How can I get it so when selecting Remote it comes through correctly the first time without having to select another option first?

entry.title: Re: Understanding and Documenting the Communication Flow in Remote table, Replication and Dataflow
entry.content: Would it be possible to check the URLs? None of them seem to work for me.

entry.title: Understanding The Cost of Supply Chain Disruption
entry.content:
Supply chains are the backbone of many industries and businesses. Every part, from raw materials to operational technology, must arrive on time and meet the exact specifications to keep production running smoothly. When supply chain disruption strikes, the impacts are devastating. In 2021, during the height of post-COVID supply chain disruptions, the average cost of supply chain disruptions for an organization was $228 million according to Statista. 

Factors such as persistent inflation and high interest rates mean that supply chain risks are still alive and well today. According to Deloitte, the leading cause of supply chain issues in Central Europe was higher prices for commodities. This is because the majority of suppliers are private companies with finite resources, so, when inflationary pressures hit, their cash reserves are squeezed and they become less resilient against unforeseen disruption. 

In today’s economic landscape, how can companies gauge risk levels across their entire supply chain? Through financial health monitoring.  

By analyzing the financial statements of their suppliers, organizations understand whether their suppliers have the resources to withstand unexpected challenges. And they can take proactive steps to mitigate risk. At a broader level, companies can observe risk in portfolios or categories of suppliers and detect risk concentrations effectively.   

RapidRatings analysis of supplier financials across four key sectors in manufacturing shows where risk is heavily concentrated: 

  • Automotive: The bulk of the automotive supply chain is Transportation Equipment Manufacturing, making up about 18% of the supply chain. However,  RapidRatings data shows that 30% of suppliers in this sector are high risk or very high risk. 
  • Healthcare and Pharma: One of the most critical supplier sectors to the pharma and healthcare industries is Scientific Research and Development Services, as it drives and innovation and development in drugs and devices. Concerningly, 24% of these suppliers are at high risk or very high risk based on their ability to withstand financial pressure and other challenges.   
  • Aerospace and Defense: Aerospace Product and Parts Manufacturing is a vital segment in the aerospace and defense supply chain, yet 36% of suppliers have a high or very high risk of defaulting – representing a substantial level of exposure to disruption.  
  • Technology: Close to 20% of the semiconductor and other electronic component manufacturing sector are high or very high risk based on analysis of their financial health. 

By implementing comprehensive financial health monitoring, organizations can identify high risk suppliers and manage their exposure to disruption risk. RapidRatings’ FHR is a financial health assessment that provides ratings on private and public suppliers using financial statements, giving companies a comprehensive view of risk levels across their entire supply chain  

Understanding supplier’s financial health is not solely about avoiding or reducing exposure to risk, but about building strategic partnerships. Financial assessments allow you to partner with strong suppliers, increase longer-term commitments, and create mutual growth opportunities.  

In today's volatile economic climate, supply chain disruption is a constant threat. By prioritizing financial health monitoring and building robust supplier relationships, businesses can significantly reduce their exposure to risk and gain a competitive advantage.

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: Hidden Gems – SAP Watch List Screening for Simplified Due Diligence and Compliance
entry.content:
Hi @Henry_Banks, 

Thank you for your question.

As highlighted in the Supplemental Terms and Conditions: "The Customer is required to maintain an active license to the Mendel Verlag content during the entire Term of the Cloud Service". This is because SAP Watch List Screening uses third-party content provided by Mendel Verlag and that content packages are not included in the Cloud Service.

I trust this clarifies.

Kind regards, 

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Help Needed: Updating Target API Level to 34 for Google Play Compliance
entry.content: Any update on this?

entry.title: Re: How to create reports for Concur report ID and financ...
entry.content:
Hello,

The "Concur Integration Setup" App is for you to maintain the connection between your SAP Concur and SAP S/4HANA Cloud systems & through Concur Posting Documents Monitor App, you can monitor all documents imported into that system. This app displays the status of all documents, the payment status when possible, if attachments are available, and any customer modifications that were done. 

To find the expense report IDs, you can use the ‘Display Line Items in General Ledger’ app.
Here is the documentation. Use the filters to select the relevant company code, fiscal year, and posting date. This will display the line items in the general ledger that are associated with the specified Concur expense report ID. You can then view the details of the financial postings for each line item.

Thank you!
Cryssal

entry.title: Enable Email Automation in C4C
entry.content:
Hello Developers!!!

I hope you’re doing well.

This blog is all about to sent a specific C4C report on a certain interval to a list of recipients in the system. We would be using the standard feature Broadcast in Business Analytics work center.

Requirement

The Client team would like to receive the C4C licensed user's last log-on date on a monthly basis. The report should include the below fields.

1. Employee Name                                        3. Employee Email

2. Employee Technical Name                        4. Last Logon Date

Resolution

In order to achieve this; firstly we have to create a custom Data Source which can fetch the relevant connected data. To achieve this you can join the below Data Sources in the below format. 

1. Employee Master Data Source & User Subscription (DS1)

2. DS1 + User Logon Details (DS2)

Note: You can do all this in one Data Source; but as the data source was being used for other reports as well; we have created a new Data Source.

Once the data source is ready; you can use the final Data source (DS2) to create a report. Ensure you also set fixed selection to the below fields as below to reduce the data set & loading time of the report so as to get good performance.

Once you have finished the enhancement; you can start to creating selection and views to the report.

Note: The data is masked due to privacy concerns.

Later you can create a broadcast from Business Analytics -> Broadcasts.

 

In the detail screen you can add relevant details like below.

Once the format, report view & selection and frequency is set; you can save and close the broadcast. One of the most important this is that you have to assign the report to all the recipients business role else the broadcast would fail throwing access error.

Once that's done you can go to the Broadcasts listing screen and start the Broadcast like below.

Resolution

The above steps were implemented and later the broadcast has started working. Once the Broadcast is started you will receive an email from the SAP System with the report. Thus, the automation was achieved.

Hope you enjoyed the blog. We have a lot more coming, so do like and follow for more such content.

Bye bye!! 😊 

Ananthu R Biju

Senior C4C Consultant

entry.title: How is the material document header text transferred when performing a PGR for an inbound delivery?
entry.content:
Hi...

How is the material document header text transferred when performing a PGR for an inbound delivery (IBD) when the stock is moved to EWM?
We perform the receipt in an IM sloc and then use a standard 311 movement to move the stock to the EWM managed sloc.
We need help to understand if there is any setup/override rule that controls what value should be transferred when we perform a PGR with reference to IBD.

Thanks

entry.title: BPA Automation doesnt stop running,
entry.content:
Hey, I have the following problem. I'm doing a sap developers tutorial.

 

 

 

 

 

When testing the automated process, it works perfectly, extracting the order data I need.

When I deploy and test the entire process completely, from the workflows panel it is evident that the automated process is reached and started, but it never ends and obviously it does not extract the information I need. I'm almost completely sure that I followed the tutorial to the letter and it still gives me that error.

Help please

entry.content:

Cont. type 5, prod. cat. 560 prod. type Z20, trans. type  not defined

Message no. B0290

I revisited the configuration steps, and I still don't know what the reason is for this error.

could you please help me?

SAP Treasury and Risk Management  SAP S/4HANA business partner

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA 2.0 installation error SLES 12 2

As I said, it was most likely some NFS artefact, but I didn't see the need to hunt the problem down once I had resolved it.

entry.title: Is the field Commercial Price editable?
entry.content:
We would like to know if the field Commercial Price can be populated or is static.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is the field Commercial Price editable?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


Yes, the field Commercial Price 1 in Accounting is editable and can be transferred from inventory cost estimates for the material. This field is a valuation price and can be updated via API:

Valuation Account Data
Master Data of Process Orders


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Solution Manager 7.2 - System recommendations tile not displaying
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a very weird problems. When I launch transaction sm_workcenter and click the System Recommendations tile, I am getting this wicked error message:

Here is the full text from the developer console:

2024-08-20 23:37:29.865000 The issue is most likely caused by application sap.dfa.help.utils.adapters.fiori. Please create a support incident and assign it to the support component of the respective application. - Failed to load UI5 component with properties: '{
"url": "/resources/sap/dfa/help/utils/adapters/fiori",
"self": {
"name": "sap.dfa.help.utils.adapters.fiori"
},
"enabled": true,
"loaded": true,
"name": "sap.dfa.help.utils.adapters.fiori",
"asyncHints": {
"preloadBundles": [
"sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/core-ext-light-0.js",
"sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/core-ext-light-1.js",
"sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/core-ext-light-2.js",
"sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/core-ext-light-3.js"
]
},
"componentData": {
"startupParameters": {},
"config": {
"product": "SAP_SOLUTION_MANAGER",
"version": "7.2 SPS 18",
"Product": "false"
},
"oPostMessageInterface": {}
},
"async": true
}'. Error likely caused by:
makeNestedError@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:50:8121

r@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:50:21707
z/</<@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/core-min-2.js:96:30214

[5]</E.dispatch@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:285:6396
x/X.handleEvent@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:285:37048
h@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:285:37382
x/X.send@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:285:39699
c/window.XMLHttpRequest.prototype[s]@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:249:312

r@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:50:21707
z/</<@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/core-min-2.js:96:30214

w/<@https://<hostname>:50001/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell/resources/~20230802110000~/sap/ushell_abap/bootstrap/evo/abap.js:156:564
sap.dfa.help.utils.adapters.fiori abap.js:63:2480

 

I have tried runnig these 2 reports:

/UI5/APP_INDEX_CALCULATE
/UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES

and cleaning the browser cache, nothing helped 😞

The ICF service /sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell is active!

Other SAP notes or threads which I found are about GL and mention ICF services to be activated which are not present in a Solution Manager system. I checked and I wasn't able to find them. Please advise!

entry.title: Re: How to implement a version-driven date filter in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content: Hi, how can we include unbooked data on this calculated dimension? I have the following calculated dimension:

entry.title: Re: How to implement a version-driven date filter in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content: Hi, how can we include unbooked data on this calculated dimension? I have the following calculated dimension: [d/"Model_1":Date].[p/CALMONTH] in order to first group the dates by calendar month. But unfortunately this only applies a calendar month dimension member to booked data, not unbooked. Is this possible?

entry.title: Executive Review alerts for budget overspend
entry.content:
Introduction

In SuccessFactors Compensation module cumulative budget movements (from a spend perspective – not from an available budget perspective) are handled through a feature called Executive Review where high level planners can make recommendation adjustments on their scope of hundreds or thousands of employees all in one screen.

Even though the leading practice use of individual guidelines with hard stops prevents extreme budget overspends there can be still major gaps in case the guideline brackets are generous, for example if the recommendation is 3% to 5% but the max on which the hard stop runs is 10%.

A legitimate question we often get from customers is how to make sure Executive Reviewers are alerted about budgets overspends since there is currently no feature to disallow a Save on overspend in Executive Review (it is only available in the worksheet experience which is only used by first and sometimes second level planners).

Even if there was an enhancement to allow this in the future in my opinion it would most likely be the addition of a “warning” feature with customizable popup message as any organization would still want C-Level executives to go over budget if needed.



Workaround available

One quick configuration allowing for a customized message to be displayed to Executive Reviewers consists in using custom validations at the employee level to trigger warning messages.

This workaround can only be leveraged if the compensation template uses a leading practice budget design (in my experience the vast majority of clients do) with a budget percentage assigned to each employee (based on customized attributes such as legal entity, division, country… ) and then multiplied by their annual salary to make up an individual budget envelope (which then gets summed up dynamically when filtering through Executive Review).

Mathematically the only way an executive reviewer can overspend on any given scope is if at least one employee has a merit increase greater than their calculated individual budget envelope.

Consequently, the custom validation formula will compare the merit amount for each employee vs the calculated individual budget envelope and display a message on Save such as “This employee’s merit recommendation is greater than their allocated budget envelope. Please make sure you are not overspending on the global scope.”  

 

Configuration steps

  1. Create new custom validation column (leave column name blank but keep column visible)
  2. Formula row should have something like : If(merit > Individual_budget_enveloppe,"*","")
  3. Custom validation formula row should have something like : If(merit > Individual_budget_enveloppe,"false","true"
  4. Select Warning only
  5. Add the message that should be displayed

 

 

Configuration screenshot: 

 

Warning message displayed to Executive Reviewers on Save:

 

All the best, 

Xavier

 

 

(If you found this blog useful please consider giving it a Like!)

entry.title: Re: Count function in webi
entry.content: Thank you Michael. This has solved my problem.

entry.title: Re: More Enhancements for SAP Community
entry.content:
Hi, @fabianlupa:

Thank you for these additional details. We have begun an investigation on our side.

Kind regards,

--Jerry

entry.title: Let’s Fill the Office with Action Figures!
entry.content: My geekiest hobby is collecting action figures in the office, and I think it’s the perfect place to share this passion. I love the idea of the office being a place to have fun, not just to work. That’s why I enjoy everyone bringing their own figures, balls to toss around (in a friendly way), and together creating a relaxed and enjoyable environment. Recently, we came up with a new rule: every time you grab a coffee, you have to pull a piece from the Jenga tower we have in the office. And, of course, if you’re the one who makes the tower fall, you have to do a funny challenge. The best part is that everyone brings their own toys and figures, so now the office is filled with fun things that reflect each person’s personality. I love how these little games and personal touches make the workday more enjoyable and help us bond as a team. In the end, it’s all about having a good time and making the office a place where everyone loves to be.

entry.title: Troubleshooting of Performance Issues Between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Datasphere
entry.content:
In this blog, we address the steps needed to be able to troubleshoot SAC and Datasphere performance issues.

Content structure:

  • Live Data Connection from SAP Analytics Cloud to Datasphere references
  • How to process Datasphere Performance cases
  • How to start investigation of performance issue in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
  • How to analyze/identify performance issue with HAR file (Network/Performance tab in Developer tools)
  • Run the contents of Payload tab (INA request/MDS)
  • PlanViz trace generation
  • Resultset Limit in SAC and Datasphere
  • How to Troubleshoot OData Connection issue from SAC to DS
  • How to check performance from SAP HANA Cockpit embedded

To access each topic, refer to the following Support Content Help: Troubleshooting of Performance Issues Between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Datasphere | SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: Generating SAP Fiori table maintenance dialogs in SAP S/4HANA 2021
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer , Firstly thanks for sharing this reusable object to fast track dev.

I am trying to leverage the same to get multi line and TR option for custom customizing tables.

I downloaded the rap genrator and was also able to follow the steps to create New Project ( except the implemntation type - i only see option managed and not manged semantic) , without enabling the business config app in the project.

However when the job finished and projects were generates got a few errors(like usage of strict in the behavior definitions was giving an error so commented) , which i managed to resolve but the behavior implementation class is still empty and on creating service binding type v4 and previewing the app , it doesnt show create,delete , add row , tr option.

Heres the screenshot of the app generated using rap generator . Also m on S4 2022 on-prem version. I do see Generate Objects option in ADT , but that still doesn't help me to enable multi line like copy paste form excel as this allows.

Any insights would be really helpful . Thanks!

entry.title: SSCUI 102139 to define DME user ID is unavailable.
entry.content:
Hi Friends.

We configured the SSCUI 102139 on CBC but now, after the upgrade to 2408, this SCCUI is unavailable.

I didn't find any information about deprecated of this SSCUI.

Scope itens 1EG and 2RP are activated.

CBC version 2402

CBC version 2408 

Someone knows if these SSCUI was deprecated or changed by other SSCUI.

Thanks!

Best Regards

Fabio Cassiano

entry.title: Using SAP Cloud Integration to Consume/Connect to Datasphere OData API with OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
In this document, we address a compatibility challenge between two products: Datasphere and
SAP Cloud Integration. Datasphere exclusively supports Authorization Code grant for OAuth2
authentication, while SAP Cloud Integration does not extend this support to its HTTP/OData
adapters. This discrepancy can pose a barrier to establishing connectivity between SAP Cloud
Integration and Datasphere OData API. However, by following the detailed instructions outlined
in this document, you can successfully navigate this challenge and achieve seamless integration
between these two systems.

 

Table of contents
Creating CI security material and Datasphere OAuth client........................................................................... 3
Creating an Iflow that connects to the Datasphere OData API ...................................................................... 8
Troubleshooting common errors.....................................................................................................................11
Further reading/documentation.......................................................................................................................12

To access the whitepaper, refer to the following Support Help Content URL: 
How-To Consume Datasphere OData Service in SAP Cloud Integration with OAuth 2.0 | SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: BPA for ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi Arun,

Firstly, thanks for your response. But I am looking for a custom process automation, not the pre-delivered processes.

I need a custom process automation to build and this is for SAP Business ByDesign. The need is this

1. Trigger a Bot (Planning to have this scheduled for a particular time in the day to trigger and run)

2. Query the Projects (SOAP Web services) in ByDesign for a specific list of projects to consider for automation

3. Connect to a Sharepoint site and locate an Excel that has a list of employees to be assigned to the project

4. Loop in the project from the query service and assign the team found in the Excel to those project headers

5. Set a KUT extension that the team is assigned for the users to review and release the project

Can the above process can be achieved via BPA?

I need the BoT to trigger automatically on a daily or periodic basis (time frame yet to be decided) and once the BoT is triggered the above process to be automated by the BoT.

how can I achieve the above process automation?

Attended Bot or Unattended Bot?

API-based Bot or UI Navigation Bot?

Is SOAP supported or ODATA supported in BPA?

 

Our goal is to achieve this in the backend without having any interruptions to the end users or anyone from the team.

Any content or materials, video references, or blogs that help me to develop this process automation is helpful for us.

Please help and appreciate your support and guidance here.

 

Thank you,

Hanu

entry.title: Re: Using Responsibility Rules in Situation Handling (2/2)
entry.content:
This is great Blog. Thanks.

Do you have any documentation about how to create custom Responsabily Context and Rules? I have manage to create one, but I have to hardcode my parameters values becuase I couldn't do the mapping. The Data Source Field is not allowing me to put my CDS View on it, so I guess I have to assign that in another screen.

 

Best Regards:

 

Luis Lara.

entry.title: Re: HANA System Replication - Bring up Secondary host to Production Mode without execute sr_takeover
entry.content: pay attention to topology, it got screwed after following these steps, I need to re create it.

entry.title: BPA - Process Automation for Project Team Assignments
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am looking for custom process automation to build and this is for SAP Business ByDesign. The need is this as follows

1. Trigger a Bot (Planning to have this scheduled for a particular time in the day to trigger and execute)

2. Query the Projects (SOAP Web services) in ByDesign for a specific list of projects to consider for automation

3. Connect to a Sharepoint site and locate an Excel that has a list of employees to be assigned to the project

4. Loop in the project from the query service response (step 1) and assign the team found in the Excel to those project headers

5. Set a flag (extension field) on the header that the team is assigned for the users to review and release the project

Can the above process be achieved via BPA for ByDesign?

I need the BoT to trigger automatically on a daily or periodic basis (time frame yet to be decided) and once the BoT is triggered the above process steps to be automated by the BoT.

how can I achieve the above process automation?

Attended Bot or Unattended Bot?

API-based Bot or UI Navigation Bot?

Is SOAP supported or ODATA supported in BPA?

 

Our goal is to achieve this in the backend without having any interruptions to the end users or anyone from the team. We are stuck on the flow and implementing this, need expert advice and steps to fulfill our need.

Any content or materials, video references, or blogs that help me to develop this process automation is helpful for us.

Please help and appreciate your support and guidance here.

Thank you,

Hanu

entry.title: Re: How To Upload Data to a Public or Private Planning Version Using a File Upload Custom Widget Wi
entry.content:
Hi @AmandaJMurphy ,

Thank you for your contribution with this widget, with the 2.0.9 update I have been experiencing the following problem

When I try to import an xlsx file with several sheets it does not allow me to select the sheets, if you look at the image you can see that the drop-down menu to select the sheets is in the background and it is not possible to interact with it

Best Regards, 

Steven

entry.title: Re: Not able to see items in My Inbox Fiori App 1610
entry.content: Did you find a solution?

entry.title: Re: Enhancing SAP CPI with Custom Payload Logging using S4HANA On Prem and a UI5 Dashboard
entry.content: Excelent!

entry.title: Re: E_ACTAI_2403-SAP Certified Specialist – Project Manager
entry.content: Hi all, I'm in the midst of deciding to go for either the SAP Certified Specialist - SAP Activate Agile Implementation or the SAP Certified Associate - SAP Activate Project Manager. The Organisation that I'm with implementing SAP mainly prefers to go agile with their projects, however I do see that the materials in the Certified SAP Associate PM course do touch on agile methodology as well. Would it be beneficial for me to go for both exams? TIA

entry.title: Re: Green field vs Brown field approach with Margin analy...
entry.content:
Dear, Costing based COPA is still supported in S/4HANA OP or private version.  2609491 - Profitability analysis in S/4HANA 

There is no migration tool to migrate costing based COPA to account based(Margin Analysis) in S/4HANA.

Regards,

Sen

entry.title: 🟢 Data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities
entry.content:
Data access patterns for ABAP-managed data can be categorized into data federation and data replication, as well as privileged access and business user access. Data federation allows for live access to the source system without replicating the data, while data replication involves replicating the data to a second system for local execution.
In this session, we present steps for external data access from an ABAP System via SQL services and ABAP CDS based external entities. Data replication support with ABAP SQL service is also presented.

Speakers : Arne Harren and Harish Bokkasam

entry.title: Make Putaway quantity Field mandatory in Inbound delivery
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have a requirement of making "Putaway quantity" field mandatory in Inbound delivery.

Kindly provide possible way to make the field mandatory in the SAP S/4HANA Public cloud.

Thanks.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Make Putaway quantity Field mandatory in Inbound deli...
entry.content: Please check the SSCUI 'Define Delivery Item Categories'(102974) for the corresponding item category. If the relevant for picking indicator is ticked, it is mandatory to do putaway before posting goods issue for the inbound delivery.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Datasphere Consumption APIs in SAP Build - A 3-legged Authorization Flow Setup
entry.content:
Hi @gustavokath ,

In my use case trying to use grant type OAuth2AuthorizationCode is pretty much useless if you want to use remote service in CAP service when you want to use a service user to do calls to Datasphere. The amount of effort to write a wrapper for it work is just silly for something that should work out of the box.

Also, I cannot use SAML assert since my calls happen in the background.

In DS if you set the API Access to "Client Credentials" then it redirects you to IAS and forces you to do SAML assert. Why is it that in the DS docs also say that only "Interactive Use" is supported when there is an option for client credentials? 

What am I missing here?

Regards,
Lochner

entry.title: Re: S4/Hana Group Reporting: Correct data on Soctypes for...
entry.content:
Dear,

You may consider manually post group reporting documents via Fiori app Import Group Journal Entries

entry.title: Re: SAP Design Studio Custom Community components aren't working after BWQ refresh??
entry.content: Has anyone ran into this type of issue after a system refresh from PROD?? That’s the only thing different so I’m curious if there is a configuration that is still pointing to our PROD instance that basis might have missed.

entry.title: Re: AYT-53648 - Canvas and Table report deprecation
entry.content: I recommend to keep Tables report instead of deprecating it. It simple to configure and run.

entry.title: Re: AYT-53648 - Canvas and Table report deprecation
entry.content: I recommend to keep Tables report instead of deprecating it. It is the bread and butter reporting. It simple to configure and run. People Stories on the other hand is an extremely difficult tool to learn, configure and run to customer's expectations. Moreover, it is extremely slow, and prone to errors.

entry.title: Re: How to create a list of SAP HANA parameters
entry.content: Thank you for your reply. I checked Note.2186744, but it's difficult for me to understand. Example) I executed the following SQL statement in SystemDB and TenantDB. hdbsql SYSTEMDB=> select * from SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS where FILE_NAME = 'global.ini' and KEY = 'global_auditing_state' FILE_NAME,LAYER_NAME,TENANT_NAME,HOST,SECTION,KEY,VALUE "global.ini","DEFAULT","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state"," FALSE" hdbsql TH1=> select * from SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS where FILE_NAME = 'global.ini' and KEY = 'global_auditing_state' FILE_NAME,LAYER_NAME,TENANT_NAME,HOST,SECTION,KEY,VALUE "global.ini","SYSTEM","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state","true" "global.ini","DATABASE","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state","true" "global.ini","DEFAULT","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state","FALSE"

entry.title: Custom APIs/Modifying standard Apis
entry.content:
I have a requirement which I have to call SAP API from an external agent via POST only. I have identified a similar SAC Api, /v1/scim/User/{id} , which has similar functionality but does not fulfil the requirement. For the API call requirement, it should be something like the following.

POST /getUser/{id} - > Get user details based on id
POST /deleteUser/{id} - > Delete user based on id
POST /updateUser/{id} -> Update user based on id

Is this achievable in SAC or is there a custom workaround to it.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to create a list of SAP HANA parameters
entry.content:
Thank you for your reply.

I checked Note.2186744, but it's difficult for me to understand.

Example)
I executed the following SQL statement in SystemDB and TenantDB.

hdbsql SYSTEMDB=> select * from SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS where FILE_NAME = 'global.ini' and KEY = 'global_auditing_state' FILE_NAME,LAYER_NAME,TENANT_NAME,HOST,SECTION,KEY,VALUE "global.ini","DEFAULT","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state"," FALSE"

hdbsql TH1=> select * from SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS where FILE_NAME = 'global.ini' and KEY = 'global_auditing_state' FILE_NAME,LAYER_NAME,TENANT_NAME,HOST,SECTION,KEY,VALUE "global.ini","SYSTEM","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state","true" "global.ini","DATABASE","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state","true" "global.ini","DEFAULT","","","auditing configuration","global_auditing_state","FALSE"

In the above case, how should we think about the parameters of each database?

If the LAYER_NAME in the SQL output of TenantDB is 'SYSTEMDB', will it be the parameter applied to systemDB?

If the LAYER_NAME in the SQL output of TenantDB is 'DATABASE', will it be the parameter applied to TenantDB?

The value 'DEFAULT' is output for LAYER_NAME in the SQL output of both SystemDB and TenantDB, but shouldn't they basically be the same?

Also, if there is a parameter with LAYER_NAME registered as 'SYSTEMDB' in both systemDB and TenantDB, which DB's parameter set will be valid for systemDB

entry.title: Re: Which CDS view is the corresponding MD04?
entry.content: API_MRP_MATERIALS_SRV_01 用这个读

entry.title: 回复： SAP RAP - Determination of on modify is not triggered...
entry.content:
Hi, As you see. The feature only supported in draft apps.

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/a90c55840b144f2ebc2d836adbc1a54f

entry.title: 回复： Horizontal scroll in RAP
entry.content: Hi, the report you show is constructed with a responsiveTable by default, you need to change the table type to GridTable.

entry.title: SAP SD Pricing Access Sequence
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently working on updating the SD Pricing Access Sequence and would like to make sure we have considered all aspects during development and testing so current operations will not be jeopardized.

I would appreciate it if you can share your thoughts on what should be considered, including the risks and other factors involved.

Thank you.

Jodie Feliz Racho

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome!
entry.content: Looking forward for devtober fest

entry.title: Re: SAP SD Pricing Access Sequence
entry.content: Hi - This seems to be a very broad question. If you are updating existing access sequence and adding new condition tables, you should probably consider the sequence of the condition tables that are in the access sequence. if you have interfaces that are dependent on sending information from these tables, that may also be one of the considerations. Maintenance of data with the new condition table would also be a possible consideration. However, unless you have more information or specifics, it is probably difficult to give a specific answer to your question.

entry.title: Re: Standard price wants to change several times in a month
entry.content: Can you share the solution?

entry.title: Re: Why is doing batch without stock while doing POGI?
entry.content:
@mdgolamrabbani 

Please check and share your Batch Determination setup. It should be based on your settings.

entry.title: Re-imbursement Codes for Europe
entry.content:
Hi

We are in the process of implementing S/4 HANA for a client that sell medical products. One of the requirements is that every line item of the invoice also carries the re-imbursement code that is provided by the Ministry of Healthcare for that country. The codes are different by country.

Would appreciate any insights from team members on how this may have been handled. 

Appreciate your help

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP NetWeaver 7.4 SP8 - Optimized for SAP HANA, Cloud and Mobile - Service Release 2 available n
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Additionally, it would help if you include the link for NetWeaver 7.4-NW-VSI for configuration; and confirms a AS ABAP and Java is required for the Virus Scan Server.

And sample links of NW-VSI landscapes, thanks in advance.

🙂

entry.title: What is the best Project Structure -> Scenario -> Customer Project executed with different companies
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I'm looking your advice to define the best Project Structure considering the following scenario:

  • Same CO Area
  • Different Company Codes

A new incoming Customer Project has been executed with two companies in our organization, localized in different countries.  One of these two companies is the one that has the main contract with the customer (Company A). 

Company A must execute part of this project but ask for help to Company B. Both companies need to manage materials so networks must be necessary.

What is the best solution for the project structure you suggest?

Two projects with their own wbs structure or one project with two WBS structures?

Thanks

entry.content: Unlock a world of interesting opportunities with Bit starz "56789"! This exclusive code allows you to access great rewards and free spins, allowing you to enhance your gaming experience. Here, everything you need to know to make the most of this bonus code.
